tools.name: query-blueprints
tools.description: Queries available blueprints.

tools.name: enable-blueprints
tools.description: Enables blueprints in the current conversation by their keys. Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled. Always call [query-blueprints] first to discover available blueprint keys.

keys.description: The keys of the blueprints to enable.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query available blueprints and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-blueprints] to refresh the list of available blueprints.
  Use [enable-blueprints] to enable blueprints by their keys. Always call [query-blueprints] first.
  Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_blueprints>
  - org.dxos.blueprint.blueprint-manager "Blueprint Manager" -- Query and enable blueprints in the current conversation.
  - org.dxos.blueprint.database "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  - org.dxos.blueprint.markdown "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. [agent-can-enable]
  - org.dxos.blueprint.research "Research" -- Researches the web and creates structured data.
  </available_blueprints>
</blueprint>
Enable the Markdown blueprint and then create a markdown document called "Cookie Recipe" with a simple chocolate chip cookie recipe.

params.name: Cookie Recipe
params.content:
# Chocolate Chip Cookies

A classic, simple recipe for delicious homemade chocolate chip cookies.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. **Mix dry ingredients:** In a bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla extract.
5. **Combine:** Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until just combined.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula.
7. **Scoop and bake:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto ungreased baking sheets. Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until golden brown.
8. **Cool:** Let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 2 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

## Tips

- Don't overmix the dough — this keeps cookies soft and tender.
- For chewier cookies, use more brown sugar than granulated sugar.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.

**Yield:** ~5 dozen cookies | **Prep time:** 15 min | **Bake time:** 9–11 min

params.name: Cookie Recipe
params.content:
# Chocolate Chip Cookies

A classic, simple recipe for soft and chewy chocolate chip cookies.

## Ingredients

- 2¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. **Mix dry ingredients:** In a bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla extract.
5. **Combine:** Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until just combined.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula.
7. **Scoop and bake:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto ungreased baking sheets. Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
8. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 2 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.

## Tips

- For chewier cookies, use more brown sugar than white sugar.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.
- Don't overbake — the cookies will firm up as they cool!

**Yield:** ~5 dozen cookies | **Prep Time:** 15 min | **Bake Time:** 9–11 min

Enjoy! 🍪
